headline: Trinidad and Tobago FIRST BASELINE CONFIRM at 48.4 / Functional — standard rotation reconstruction. Structural evidence: ~44,800 Venezuelan migrants/refugees/asylum seekers (highest per-capita in Caribbean) with no asylum-specific legislation; irregular entry criminalized; UNHCR-documented deportations including registered asylum seekers (UNHCR characterized as breach of international refugee law); 2024 court order awarded a Venezuelan boy and mother USD 2.4M in damages for 456-day detention with documented mistreatment. 90+ TT nationals (50+ children) in arbitrary detention in northeast Syria for alleged ISIS links — unresolved for multiple years. Ongoing LGBTQ+ discrimination, violence against women. ICCPR follow-up due November 2026. No new in-window (May 9-10) events. Stable governance baseline with persistent rights gaps; 48.4 (Functional) plausible. Clean reconstruction.
key_evidence: Structural: ~44,800 Venezuelan migrants/refugees/asylum seekers in TT (highest per-capita in Caribbean as of 2024). No asylum-specific legislation; irregular entry treated as criminal offense.

UNHCR press release: regret over deportation of 82 Venezuelan nationals including registered asylum-seekers and individuals declaring intention to apply — characterized as breach of international refugee law.

2024 court order: Venezuelan boy and mother awarded USD 2.4M damages for 456-day detention with insufficient food/water, overcrowding, and exposure to sexual acts — judicial-system accountability functional but state-level immigration response pattern persists.
Two detention facilities: Immigration Detention Centre (IDC) and Chaguaramas Heliport (designated 2023).
90+ TT nationals including 50+ children in arbitrary detention in northeast Syria for alleged ISIS links — multi-year unresolved diplomatic-protection gap.
Ongoing LGBTQ+ discrimination and violence against women per Amnesty International country reporting.
ICCPR follow-up cycle due November 2026 — forward institutional engagement signal.

evidence.source: Amnesty International — Trinidad and Tobago country page
evidence.finding: Structural overview of LGBTQ+ discrimination, violence against women, refugee protection gaps.

evidence.source: CCPR Centre — Trinidad and Tobago consultation framework
evidence.finding: ICCPR engagement framework; follow-up due November 2026; institutional accountability channel active.

evidence.source: UNHCR — UNHCR regret at deportations of Venezuelans
evidence.finding: Deportations of 82 Venezuelans including registered asylum seekers characterized as breach of international refugee law.

evidence.source: Migration Policy Institute — Trinidad and Tobago grapples with Venezuelan migrants
evidence.finding: Structural context: ~44,800 Venezuelan migrants; no asylum-specific legislation; per-capita highest Caribbean.
Detailed documentation of refugee protection gaps; structural-reform agenda mapped.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained at published. A1 detection: judicial-system recognition of detention harm (2024 USD 2.4M ruling); A4 amplification: civil society and UNHCR engagement. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained at published. E1 affective resonance: judicial system awarded substantial damages for child detention harm; E2 protected scope: refugee-population access gaps cap upward movement. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.0 sustained at published. AC1 responsiveness: judicial system functional on individual remedy; AC4 resource mobilization: refugee-protection structural gaps persist. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained at published. EQ1 universality: refugees/asylum seekers excluded from formal protection; EQ2 categories: LGBTQ+ discrimination; EQ3 bias awareness: structural-reform proposals exist but unimplemented. Anchor 2-3 boundary at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 sustained at published. B5 multilateral engagement: ICCPR engagement cycle active; B3 scope clarity: regional engagement (Caribbean cluster) sustained. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained at published. AB4 accountability-to-external-norms: UNHCR engagement, ICCPR cycle, judicial accountability on individual cases; AB1 harm-acknowledgment: 2024 court ruling acknowledged detention harm. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained at published. S2 long-term-impact: judicial-system accountability creates precedent; S4 structural critique: refugee-system and Syria-detention gaps documented in international advocacy. Anchor 3 sustained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained at published. I1 consistency: judicial accountability on individual detention case + ongoing deportation pattern = mixed-but-sustained values-action engagement; I2 non-performance: structural-reform engagement via ICCPR cycle. Anchor 3 sustained.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 48.4 = published. Sub-threshold drift (0.0). Functional band sustained. Not a boundary case.

negativeSignals: 44,800+ Venezuelan migrants with no asylum-specific legislation; documented deportations of registered asylum seekers (UNHCR breach finding); 456-day child detention case (judicial remedy granted but pattern persists); 90+ nationals (50+ children) in arbitrary Syria detention for multi-year unresolved; LGBTQ+ discrimination; violence against women.
positiveSignals: Judicial system delivered substantive remedy on individual detention harm (2024 USD 2.4M ruling); ICCPR engagement cycle active (Nov 2026 follow-up due); UNHCR registration channel operational; civil society and international advocacy engagement; stable democratic institutions; regional Caribbean engagement.
sector_context: Latin America / Caribbean cluster. Trinidad and Tobago's 48.4 (Functional) baseline is consistent with the mixed evidence pattern: stable governance and functional judicial system offset by structural rights gaps on refugees and diplomatic-protection failures (Syria detention).
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with structural-evidence anchor
ICCPR follow-up cycle flagged as forward institutional engagement
open_actions_carried: Watch: Syria national-detention diplomatic resolution efforts
Watch: LGBTQ+ rights and violence-against-women policy progress
